bead.title: feat(work): add ddx work clear-cooldowns bulk operator command
bead.description:
PROBLEM
  Today, clearing a stale cooldown is a per-bead, per-field manual
  operation:
      ddx bead update <id> --unset execute-loop-retry-after \\
                            --unset execute-loop-last-status \\
                            --unset execute-loop-last-detail
  When a SYSTEMIC issue (e.g., a data race in executor.go) blocks
  multiple beads' pushes within minutes, each bead gets its own
  ~24h cooldown. After the systemic cause is fixed, the operator
  must clear each bead one at a time. Observed: 5+ beads required
  manual clear-loops, ~10 minutes of operator time, every time the
  systemic cause is resolved.

  A bulk operator command is needed: scan beads with a matching
  execute-loop-last-status (or all), clear their cooldown fields in
  one pass.

CURRENT STATE
  - cli/cmd/bead_cooldown_clear.go (or wherever single-bead clear
    lives) handles one bead at a time.
  - cli/internal/bead/store.go SetExecutionCooldown writes; no
    matching ClearAllCooldowns or ClearCooldownsByStatus.
  - Operators are stuck running --unset --unset --unset loops.

PROPOSED FIX
  Add a new subcommand: ddx work clear-cooldowns [flags]

  Flags:
    --all                 Clear cooldowns on every bead with
                          execute-loop-retry-after set.
    --status <S>          Clear only on beads where
                          execute-loop-last-status==<S>
                          (e.g., push_failed, no_changes_unjustified).
    --dry-run             Print what would be cleared without
                          modifying state.

  Behavior:
    - Scan beads.jsonl (under store lock) for entries with
      execute-loop-retry-after set.
    - Filter by --status if provided.
    - For each match, delete: execute-loop-retry-after,
      execute-loop-last-status, execute-loop-last-detail.
    - Emit a structured "cooldown-cleared" event per bead with
      reason="operator-bulk-clear".
    - Print summary: N cooldowns cleared (or "would clear" for
      --dry-run).

  Implementation:
    - cli/internal/bead/store.go: new method ClearCooldowns(filter
      func(Bead) bool) (int, error) — single-pass batch update.
    - cli/cmd/work_clear_cooldowns.go (new file) — CLI plumbing.
    - cli/cmd/work_clear_cooldowns_test.go: TestClearCooldowns_All
      asserts every cooldown cleared; TestClearCooldowns_ByStatus
      asserts only matching are cleared; TestClearCooldowns_DryRun
      asserts no mutation in dry-run.

NON-SCOPE
  - Auto-invalidation (sister bead handles rev-bound auto-clear).
  - Single-bead clear shortcut (existing path is fine for one-off).
  - GUI / dashboard for cooldown management.

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead "fix(try): push_failed must not set cooldown" (R1)
    reduces the FUTURE need for this command, but doesn't eliminate
    it (other cooldowns can still go stale).
  - Sister bead "feat(bead): rev-bound cooldown auto-invalidation"
    (R3) is the long-term elegant fix; this is the operator
    escape valve until that lands.
bead.acceptance:
1. cli/cmd/work_clear_cooldowns.go (or equivalent) implements `ddx work clear-cooldowns` with --all, --status, --dry-run flags.
2. cli/internal/bead/store.go contains a ClearCooldowns method (or equivalent) that batch-clears execute-loop-retry-after, execute-loop-last-status, execute-loop-last-detail under the store lock.
3. Each cleared bead has a "cooldown-cleared" event appended with reason="operator-bulk-clear".
4. TestClearCooldowns_All in cli/cmd/work_clear_cooldowns_test.go asserts all cooldowns are cleared.
5. TestClearCooldowns_ByStatus asserts only matching --status beads are cleared.
6. TestClearCooldowns_DryRun asserts no mutation when --dry-run is passed.
7. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/bead/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
